Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Quash Subpoena Issued For Case Pending In Foreign Jurisdiction Pursuant To C.C.P. 2029.600
Off Calendar Apr-19-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Quash Subpoena Issued For Case Pending In Foreign Jurisdiction Pursuant To C.C.P. 2029.600
Off Calendar Mar-26-2021 Continued To May-24-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. JEFFREY M. ROSENFELD, Esq. of Kronenberger Rosenfeld, LLP (Tel: 415-955-1155) and JOSHUA E. DUBIN, Esq. (Tel: 917-523-0124) for petitioners;
Attys. SCOTT M. DANNER, Esq. of Holwell Shuster & Goldberg LLP (Tel: 646-837-5151) and C. HUNTER HAYES, Esq. of Munger, Tolles & Olson LLP (Tel: 213-683-9100) for Respondents IAC/Interactivecorp and Match Group, Inc.

HEARING RE: PETITIONERS SEAN RAD, PAUL CAFARDO, GARETH JOHNSON, ALEXA MATEEN, JUSTIN MATEEN, AND RYAN OGLE's Petition To Quash Subpoena Issued For Case Pending In Foreign Jurisdiction Pursuant To C.C.P. 2029.600.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear.

Matter discussed.

The Court rules as follows:

The petition to quash shall be continued to a date after the New York Court rules on the motion to limit the Merrill Lynch subpoena which is set for hearing on April 15. The New York Court's ruling on whether the Merrill Lynch subpoena was served late and whether it is relevant will be helpful to this Court. The Court does not take a position at this time on whether the New York Court's ruling on lateness or on relevance will be dispositive.

Matter is continued to May 24, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. on the Discovery Calendar to be heard by the judge pro tem assigned to that date (this matter is not being retained by Judge Pro Tem Bruce Highman).

The Court and parties agree to the following supplemental briefing schedule: Supplemental moving brief limited to 4 pages is due 5/6/21; supplemental opposition brief limited to 4 pages is due 5/13/21; supplemental reply brief limited to 2 pages is due 5/17/21.

Petitioner's counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem by email sent to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Bruce Highman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR No. 13293,
Tel: 415-274-9977, Email: calendar-ca@veritext.com (302/JPT)
